Corn, soybean basis steady-to-lower, futures post weekly gains
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn and soybeans delivered to U.S. Midwest elevators and processors were mostly steady to lower on Friday as futures posted moderate weekly gains, while buyers looked ahead to heftier supplies once the harvest ramps up in the coming weeks, grain merchants said.

  • U.S. farmers will harvest a record corn crop in 2025 after ideal weather across much of the Midwest this summer, but the bounty will fall short of the U.S. government's lofty outlook as pockets of plant disease and heat stress dented yields in spots across the farm belt, crop consultancy Pro Farmer said on Friday. Growers are also expected to reap a bumper soybean crop.

  • A large soy processor in Decatur, Illinois, lowered its spot basis bid by 5 cents a bushel on Friday, joining several other crushers that have trimmed bids this week. Crush industry demand for beans has slowed this month as processing plants are taking downtime for seasonal maintenance and repairs ahead of the fall harvest.

  • An elevator in the Chicago area continued to post a quick-ship premium of 15 cents a bushel for corn delivered before next week.

  • An elevator in Council Bluffs, Iowa, rolled its corn bid from the September futures contract (CU25) to the December (CZ25), resulting in a 4-cent drop in the cash price.

  • CBOT December corn futures (CZ25) ended 1/4 cent lower at $4.11-1/2 per bushel, while November soybeans (SX25) settled 2-1/2 cents higher at $10.58-1/2 per bushel. Both were up about 1.5% for the week.

data.title: Nvidia Rolls Out Spectrum-XGS Ethernet For AI Factories
data.description:
Nvidia is giving data centers a new way to scale. The chip giant on Friday unveiled Spectrum-XGS Ethernet, a networking platform built to connect AI factories not just within one building but across cities, countries and even continents. Shares rose 1.7% in late morning trading.

CEO Jensen Huang called the launch a cornerstone for the next phase of AI growth. The AI industrial revolution is here, he said, describing Spectrum-XGS as a scale-across system that extends beyond the traditional scale-up or scale-out models, linking massive compute clusters into what he calls giga-scale AI factories.

The tech plugs directly into Nvidia's Spectrum-X platform and automatically manages latency, congestion and telemetry over long distances. The company said it nearly doubles the performance of its Collective Communications Library. Hyperscaler CoreWeave is already among the first to test the system by tying its data centers together.

The timing is key. Nvidia is set to report results on Aug. 27, with Wall Street expecting $46 billion in revenue and EPS of $1.01. Investors will be watching if the networking push strengthens Nvidia's grip on the AI infrastructure market.

data.title: Hamilton ETFs Announces August 2025 Monthly & Upcoming Semi-Monthly Cash Distributions
data.description:
TORONTO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 22, 2025--

Hamilton Capital Partners Inc. ("Hamilton ETFs") is pleased to announce the upcoming monthly and semi-monthly cash distributions for its ETFs.

Distributions may vary from period to period.

Table A includes the Hamilton ETFs that pay monthly cash distributions, all of which trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange, for the period ended August 31, 2025. data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Powell Highlights Job Market Worries, Opening Path to Rate Cut | Dow ...
data.description:
Powell Highlights Job Market Worries, Opening Path to Rate Cut

The Fed chair opened the door for rate cuts next month when he said the labor market might be softening enough to rein in inflation that is being pushed up by tariffs.

----

Dow Hits Record as Fed Chair Opens Door to Rate Cut

U.S. stocks surged, boosting the Dow to its first record close this year after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell opened the door for the central bank to cut rates next month.

----

Canada to Remove 25% Tariff on Some U.S. Goods

The move is seen as a sign of goodwill from Ottawa as it tries to reset its trading relationship with the U.S.

----

Europe's Fragile Energy Lifeline Exposed by Ukraine's Strikes in Russia

Ukraine's attacks, encouraged by President Trump, have disrupted flows and triggered angry reactions from regional leaders.

----

Canadians Tap the Brakes After Hitting the Stores in June

Indications of a pullback in retail spending by Canadians last month following a rebound in June continues a stretch of choppy trade and could signal that household consumption is cooling amid the stresses of the trade conflict with the U.S.

----

Eurozone Wage Growth Gains Pace, Likely Adding to ECB Caution

Wages in the eurozone rose at a slightly faster pace during the three months through June as unemployment remained at record lows.

----

U.K. Consumers Sunnier as BOE Cuts Rates But Economic Fears Remain

U.K. consumers felt a little better about their finances this month as the Bank of England lowered borrowing costs, though sentiment remains weak amid wider economic turmoil.

----

Japan's Inflation Stays Well Above BOJ's 2% Target

Japan's consumer inflation cooled in July, but remained well above the central bank's target of 2%, reinforcing expectations of a coming interest-rate increase.

----

The Biggest Retailers Are Thriving in the Tariff Economy

Walmart, Amazon and T.J. Maxx are beating out rivals by offering deals to stressed-out consumers.

----

Can Germany's Merz Revive the Economy? Voters Are Running Out of Patience

Polls show voters are growing restless with Friedrich Merz, raising pressure on the chancellor to deliver on the domestic stage.

----

Why Solar and Wind Power Can Thrive Without Subsidies

Rising electricity demand, in part due to AI needs, along with the increasing cost of alternatives should cushion the impact for green energy.

----

How Trump Will Decide Which Chips Act Companies Must Give Up Equity

The administration isn't eyeing equity in semiconductor companies that are increasing U.S. investment like TSMC and Micron, but may take stakes in others.

----

Peter Thiel Leads Pack of Investors Piling Into Ether

Proponents see Ethereum becoming the platform of choice for the financial world.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 PM ET: Trump Says U.S. Will Take Nearly 10% Equity Stake in Intel | JPMorgan ...
data.description:
Trump Says U.S. Will Take Nearly 10% Equity Stake in Intel

The move comes after days of discussion in Washington about options for the troubled American chip-maker's future.

----

JPMorgan to Pay $330 Million Over 1MDB Transactions

The agreement settles claims that the lender facilitated $800 million of payments related to the looting of the Malaysian sovereign-wealth fund.

----

ESPN and MLB Are Closing In on a 3-Year Pact Worth Around $1.65 Billion

After cutting short a previous agreement, the sides are in advanced talks on a new pact that would incorporate MLB.tv into ESPN's streaming service.

----

TikTok to Lay off Hundreds of U.K. Content Moderators in AI Push

The move, which will affect several hundreds out of a workforce of about 2,500 in the country, comes as other social-media companies have started to scale back teams dedicated to policing online content.

----

Waymo to Begin Testing Its Robotaxis in New York City

The self-driving car company received a permit to begin testing a limited number of autonomous vehicles in parts of Manhattan and Downtown Brooklyn.

----

BJ's Wholesale Club's Sales Slow as Shoppers' Frustrations Grow

The company's comparable-club sales rose 2.3% excluding fuel sales, below analyst expectations, as shoppers across all income levels were more stressed.

----

Deutsche Post, DHL Parcel Germany Pause Business Shipping to U.S.

DHL joins a crowd of European postal groups that are suspending standard shipments to the U.S. as the country starts charging duties on lower value parcels.

----

Cenovus Energy to Buy MEG Energy in $5.68 Billion Oil-Sands Tie-Up

Cenovus Energy said its proposed buyout of rival MEG Energy will unlock hundreds of millions of dollars in savings by consolidating their neighboring oil-sands operations in Alberta.

----

Poland Plans to Raise Tax Rate Paid by Banks

Polish bank stocks were weaker after the country's finance ministry outlined plans to increase the corporate income tax rate paid by lenders in a bid to shore up its budget.

----

Elon Musk Wanted Mark Zuckerberg to Join OpenAI Bid, Court Filing Says

A Musk-led consortium offered $97.4 billion to buy OpenAI as part of his continuing legal battle with the company he co-founded alongside Sam Altman.

----

Chili's Pays Fat Bonuses to Staff After Record-Setting Sales

Brinker International-owned chain has delivered strong profit, despite challenging year for restaurants.

----

OpenAI to Open New Delhi Office as It Bulks Up India Presence

The ChatGPT maker said it has established a legal entity in India and started hiring a local team for the office, which will be based in New Delhi.

----

Kazatomprom Posts Lower Profit, Revenue But Backs Production Guidance

While long-term Uranium prices have remained stable despite the volatility in the market, the company doesn't plan to return to 100% production levels at this time.

----

Gold Fields Earnings Boosted by Higher Prices

Net profit was $1.02 billion for the first half of 2025 compared with $389 million for the same period last year.

data.title: Club Car Wash Opens New Omaha Location With $1 MVP Wash
data.description:
Limited-Time $1 Wash Deal Helps Families Through RMHC

OMAHA, NEBRASKA / ACCESS Newswire / August 22, 2025 / Club Car Wash, one of the fastest-growing express car wash companies in the United States, is excited to announce the grand opening of its newest location at 18318 Evans St, Omaha, NE 68022.

The new Omaha site offers Unlimited Wash Memberships, powerful vacuums, complimentary microfiber towels, and a fast, efficient wash experience designed to keep vehicles looking their best in just minutes.

To celebrate the opening, Club Car Wash is offering its top-tier wash-the $25 MVP-for just $1, but only for a limited time. All proceeds will support Ronald McDonald House Charities (RMHC), a nonprofit dedicated to providing a home-away-from-home for families with children receiving medical care.

"We're thrilled to continue expanding in Nebraska and bring our services to the Omaha community," said Cory Via, Chief Operations Officer at Club Car Wash. "Opening this location is another step in our mission to deliver convenient, high-quality car care while supporting the communities we serve through incredible partnerships like RMHC."

For more information, visit www.clubcarwash.com or stop by the new Omaha location to take advantage of the limited-time offer.

Contact Information

Sarah Smith

Chief Marketing Officer

marketing@clubcarwash.com

(833) 416 - 9975

SOURCE: Club Car Wash

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Club Car Wash Hosts Back-to-School $5 Wash Weekend in Waco & Hewitt
data.description:
August 22-24 event donates $1 from every wash to Midway Education Foundation to support students and teachers

WACO, TEXAS / ACCESS Newswire / August 22, 2025 / Club Car Wash is excited to host its Back-to-School $5 Wash Weekend from August 22-24 at its Waco and Hewitt locations:

1725 S Valley Mills Dr, Waco, TX

916 N Valley Mills Dr, Waco, TX

915 N Hewitt Dr, Hewitt, TX

During this limited-time event, customers can enjoy the premium MVP wash for just $5, with $1 from every wash donated directly to Midway Education Foundation to help prepare students and teachers with the materials they need for the new school year.

The Back-to-School $5 Wash Weekend is part of Club Car Wash's continued mission to make a positive impact in the communities it serves. By partnering with Midway Education Foundation, the company provides a simple way for customers to give directly to education while also receiving a top-quality car wash.

"At Club Car Wash, giving back is at the heart of what we do. Supporting local schools during back-to-school season allows us to help provide students and teachers with the resources they need to succeed," said Collin Bartels, President of Club Car Wash

In addition to the $1 donation from every MVP wash, guests can also contribute through an open donation option, available at each participating store throughout the weekend. Every dollar collected through open donations will go directly to Midway Education Foundation.

Contact Information

Sarah Smith

Chief Marketing Officer

marketing@clubcarwash.com

(833) 416 - 9975

SOURCE: Club Car Wash

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe Return to Branson This Fall
data.description:
Branson, Missouri--(Newsfile Corp. - August 22, 2025) - The magical world of Narnia is returning to the stage with the touring premiere of The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe. Visitors will not want to miss this incredible classic brought to life by The Logos Theatre on Tour and performed in the historic Williams Event Center (formerly Welk Resort). The Pevensie children will face their greatest fears and fiercest enemies, will you join the adventure? Tickets are limited - October 31-December 13.

Sample Synopsis:

When the four Pevensie children are sent to the country to live with an eccentric professor to escape the bombings in England, little do they know what incredible adventures await. A mysterious wardrobe gives them access to the magical world of Narnia, where the evil White Witch rules, keeping the land in a constant winter. With the sacrifice and help of the great lion, Aslan, the four Pevensies fight to banish the White Witch and break her hold over the land of Narnia.

From the same team that brought you The Horse and His Boy and Prince Caspian, The Logos Theatre and world-renowned director, Nicole Chavers Stratton, presents the long-awaited original stage adaptation of C. S. Lewis's beloved classic, The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe.

The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/11602/263499_84ddf57b54166bc9_002full.jpg

About the Logos Theatre:

Established in 1971, The Academy of Arts Logos Theatre is a non-profit organization that functions year-round performing state-of-the-art productions in Upstate South Carolina as well as touring across the country with large-scale productions, camps, and workshops. Their mission is to impact the culture for Christ by using the arts in "making the Bible come alive in minds and hearts through the power of storytelling". To learn more, visit https://theacademyofarts.org/.

Run Time: 2.5 hrs.

General Pricing: $37.50-$62.50

Tickets: https://narniabranson.com/

###

Contact:

Heather Hermen

(417) 243-21322

hhermen@bransoncvb.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/263499

data.title: Tuktu Resources Ltd. Announces Stock Option Grant
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - August 22, 2025) - Tuktu Resources Ltd.  ("Tuktu" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that as a part of Tuktu's long-term incentive program, and pursuant to the terms and conditions of its stock option plan, the Company granted an aggregate of 240,000 stock options to a newly-appointed director effective as of August 22, 2025. The stock options vest between the first and third anniversary of their grant date, have an exercise price of $0.05 per common share and expire on August 22, 2030.

About Tuktu Resources Ltd.

Tuktu is a publicly traded junior oil and gas development company headquartered in Calgary, Alberta with producing oil and gas properties in southern Alberta. For additional information about Tuktu please contact:

Tuktu Resources Ltd.

1750, 444 - 5th Avenue S.W.

Calgary, Alberta T2P 2T8

Attention: Tim de Freitas, President and Chief Executive Officer (phone 403-478-0141); or Mark Smith, CFO and VP Finance (phone 403-613-9661)

Neither the TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this press release.

All amounts in this press release are stated in Canadian dollars unless otherwise specified.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/263598

data.title: ON24 Executives Engage in Stock Transactions Under Trading Plans
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Vattuone StevenChief Financial OfficerSell$42,984Form 4
Sahasi JayeshEV President, Product and CTOSell$15,057Form 4
Blackie JamesChief Revenue OfficerSell$17,116Form 4

Several executives at ON24 have recently sold shares of the company's common stock under pre-established trading plans, according to SEC Form 4 filings.

Steven Vattuone, the Chief Financial Officer, sold 8,211 shares on August 20 and 21, 2025, at weighted average prices of $5.2138 and $5.2478, respectively, totaling $42,984. Following these sales, Vattuone directly owns 626,321 shares of ON24.

Jayesh Sahasi, Executive Vice President, Product and CTO, sold 2,888 shares on August 20, 2025, at a weighted average price of $5.2138, resulting in $15,057. Post-transaction, Sahasi directly owns 754,075 shares.

Blackie James, the Chief Revenue Officer, disposed of 3,283 shares on August 20, 2025, at a weighted average price of $5.2138 per share, for a total of $17,116. James now directly owns 522,307 shares of ON24.

All transactions were executed under Rule 10B5-1 trading plans, which allow company insiders to set up a predetermined plan for buying or selling stocks at a future date.

data.title: CRA International Officer Sells Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameYellin Jonathan D
RelationshipEVP AND GENERAL COUNSEL
TypeSell
Amount$328,000
SEC FilingForm 4

Jonathan D Yellin, EVP and General Counsel of CRA International, sold 1,750 shares of Common Stock on August 20, 2025, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The transactions occurred at weighted average prices of $186.4189, $187.2999, and $188.1333, with a total sale amount of $328,000. Following these transactions, Yellin directly owns 17,323 shares of CRA International. The sales were executed in multiple transactions at varying prices.

SEC Filing: CRA INTERNATIONAL, INC. [ CRAI ] - Form 4 - Aug. 22, 2025

data.title: Ether Surges After Powell Speech Renews Rate-Cut Hopes — WSJ
data.description:
By Vicky Ge Huang

Ether, the second-largest cryptocurrency, surged 14% on Friday after Fed Chair Jerome Powell's Jackson Hole remarks renewed hopes of an interest-rate cut as soon as September.

Ether is up more than 40% so far this year, topping bitcoin's roughly 25% gain. The latest rally for both digital currencies is partly driven by a new wave of companies issuing shares and debt to stockpile the two tokens.

Bullish investors, including venture capitalist Peter Thiel, have been piling into ether, believing that the token's underlying blockchain, Ethereum, could become the platform of choice for Wall Street to offer new products and services.

Read more:

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump plans to make Cold War-era plutonium available for nuclear power
data.description:
Key points:
  • Radioactive, fissile plutonium from Cold War a headache for US
  • US wants to halt disposal of it, use 20 metric tons for fuel
  • Trump administration sees it as potential fuel for new reactors
  • Critic points out similar program failed due to costs

The Trump administration plans to make available about 20 metric tons of Cold War-era plutonium from dismantled nuclear warheads to U.S. power companies as a potential fuel for reactors, according to a source familiar with the matter and a draft memo outlining the plan.

Plutonium has previously only been converted to fuel for commercial U.S. reactors in short-lived tests. The plan would follow through on an executive order signed by President Donald Trump in May ordering the government to halt much of its existing program to dilute and dispose of surplus plutonium, and instead provide it as a fuel for advanced nuclear technologies.

The Department of Energy, or DOE, plans to announce in coming days it will seek proposals from industry, said the source who spoke on the condition of anonymity. The source cautioned that because the plan is still a draft, its final details could change pending further discussions.

The plutonium would be offered to industry at little to no cost — with a catch. Industry will be responsible for costs of transportation, designing, building, and decommissioning DOE-authorized facilities to recycle, process and manufacture the fuel, the memo said.

The details on the volume of the plutonium, industry's responsibilities in the plan and the potential timing of a U.S. announcement, have not been previously reported. The 20 metric tons would be drawn from a larger, 34-metric-ton stockpile of weapons-grade plutonium that the United States had previously committed to dispose of under a non-proliferation agreement with Russia in 2000.

The Department of Energy did not confirm or deny the Reuters reporting, saying only that the department is "evaluating a variety of strategies to build and strengthen domestic supply chains for nuclear fuel, including plutonium," as directed by Trump's orders.

Boosting the U.S. power industry is a policy priority for the Trump administration as U.S. electricity demand rises for the first time in two decades on the boom in data centers needed for artificial intelligence.

The idea of using surplus plutonium for fuel has raised concerns among nuclear safety experts who argue a previous similar effort failed.

Under the 2000 agreement, the plutonium was initially planned to be converted to mixed oxide fuel, or MOX, to run in nuclear power plants. But in 2018, the first Trump administration killed the contract for a MOX project that it said would have cost more than $50 billion.

The U.S. Energy Department holds surplus plutonium at heavily guarded weapons facilities including Savannah River in South Carolina, Pantex in Texas, and Los Alamos in New Mexico. Plutonium has a half-life of 24,000 years and must be handled with protective gear.

Until Trump's May order, the U.S. program to dispose of the plutonium has involved blending it with an inert material and storing it in an experimental underground storage site called the Waste Isolation Pilot Plant (WIPP) in New Mexico.

The Energy Department has estimated that burying the plutonium would cost $20 billion.

"Trying to convert this material into reactor fuel is insanity. It would entail trying to repeat the disastrous MOX fuel program and hoping for a different result," said Edwin Lyman, a nuclear physicist at the Union of Concerned Scientists.

"The excess plutonium is a dangerous waste product and DOE should stick to the safer, more secure and far cheaper plan to dilute and directly dispose of it in WIPP."

data.title: US stocks weekly: Doves fly
data.description:
** S&P 500 extends winning run to three weeks, finishes up 0.3% after Fed chair Powell's dovish shift on interest rates

** Dow gains 1.5%, though Nasdaq Composite eases 0.6%; on Fri, the Dow scores its first record closing high since Dec. 04, 2024

** S&P 500 index has been driving to new highs, steers clear of a Jackson pothole

** U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield falls back to the 4.25% area; still holed up in the cloud

** Nearly every sector ascends: Economically-sensitive plays lift off, while just Communication Services and Technology sputter; indeed, Growth (.IGX) looked ripe for a rest vs Value ; Growth suffers worst week relative to Value since late Mar

** Energy surges 2.8%. Group elevates as Fed chair points to possible rate cut at Jackson Hole Symposium

** Real estate soars 2.4%. REITs such as Alexandria Real Estate and Prologis rise after Powell's speech hints at rate cuts

** Financials jump 2.1%. Banks vault as Powell signals possible rate cut in Sept., but stopped short of committing

For the week, S&P 500 banks index (.SPXBK) up 3%, KBW regional banking index leaps >5%

** Industrials gain 1.8%. HR software firm Dayforce jumps 31% on the week on $12 bln Thoma Bravo buyout deal

** Consumer Discretionary up 1.3%. Home Depot rises after it maintains annual sales and earnings forecasts, flags 'modest' price hikes due to tariffs

Homebuilders D.R. Horton , Lennar and Pultegroup rally as rate cut odds increase after Powell's comments

** Consumer Staples edge up 0.3%. Walmart falls after first profit miss in about three years, even as it hikes annual forecast

And Target slumps after co names longtime insider Fiddelke as new CEO, despite Q2 results beat

** Communication Services descend 0.9%. Google-parent Alphabet rises on Fri after report Meta Platforms inks $10 bln cloud deal

** Tech tumbles 1.6%. Highflyer Palantir worst SPX performer, pulls back 10% on the week, as tech-stock stumble shows vulnerability in AI trade, and short sellers rake in bumper profits on PLTR's recent slide

Intel climbs on Tues as Japan's SoftBank chips in with $2 bln investment

Semiconductor index ends essentially flat

** Meanwhile, individual investor bears dip, but still "unusually high"

data.title: Diginex Expects Longer Timeline to Secure Listing on Abu Dhabi Exchange
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Diginex Limited said it will now take a few more months or longer to secure a dual listing of its stock on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange (ADX).

The regulatory technology company said Friday it has held multiple meetings with the United Arab Emirates and the process remains ongoing.

The company had entered a memorandum of understanding with Nomas Global Investments, a solely owned SPV of His Highness Shaikh Mohammed Bin Sultan Bin Hamdan Al Nahyan of Abu Dhabi's Royal Family, on March 17.

The agreement was intended to secure a dual-listing on the ADX, expand Diginex's business in the Gulf Cooperation Council and secure a potential capital raise of up to $250 million.

Diginex's potential capital raise will most likely occur following the listing of its shares on the ADX, the company said.

In a separate transaction in May, Nomas bought Diginex warrants to acquire 6.75 million ordinary shares from Rhino Ventures Limited, an entity owned and controlled by Diginex's Chair Miles Pelham.

The companies have mutually decided to rescind the acquisition of the Diginex warrants, effective Aug. 22. Diginex warrants previously held by Nomas will now revert back to Rhino Ventures Limited.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Brazil raw steel output falls 9.6 % in July, AÇO BRASIL says
data.description: SAO PAULO, 22 Aug (Reuters) – Raw steel output in Brazil fell 9.6 % to 2.802 million tonnes in July in an annual basis, Aco Brasil, the group that represents mills, said on Friday. Sales in the local market fell 2.9 % to 1.856 million tonnes in the July from the same period a year earlier,the institute, known as Aço Brasil, said in a report.

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1345 ET - Intuit's disappointing 1Q guidance is likely garnering extra scrutiny from investors given concerns heading into the report, including those around the rise of artificial-intelligence search, UBS analysts say in a note. The company, for its part, does not seem to be seeing any material negative impact yet, with traffic up significantly, and believes it will show up more and have higher conversion rates with AI search queries, the analysts note. The key debate remains whether Intuit's outlook is merely conservative, or actually closer to reality, they say. "At a time when prints need to be exceptional for any follow through, we're not surprised by the reaction," they say. Shares fall 4.4%, to $666.83. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1047 ET - Instacart's singular focus on groceries means it could struggle to compete with its competitors like Uber, DoorDash and Amazon that have wider offerings, say Wedbush analysts Scott Devitt and Matthew Weiss. "Instacart is at risk of losing incremental market share to other leading intermediaries that can leverage their scale and success in adjacent categories to capture demand for online grocery and convenience," the analysts say. Meanwhile, Instacart's subscription program, priced in line with its competitors' subscriptions, offers far fewer benefits. Uber One for example offers exclusive discounts, cashback incentives and mobility offerings, while Instacart+ mostly just offers reduced delivery fees, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1032 ET - Amazon's recent expansion of same-day grocery delivery makes a bad competitive environment even worse for Instacart, say Wedbush analysts Scott Devitt and Matthew Weiss, who lower their rating of the stock of Instacart parent company Maplebear to underperform. Instacart's grocery delivery market share has already fallen to 58% in 2024 from 70% in 2022 due to competition from Uber and Doordash. Now, "Amazon can meet logistical challenges faced by many grocery retailers, which have typically been addressed by third parties, such as Instacart," the analysts say. Amazon's grocery expansion makes Prime "a more compelling subscription for grocery shoppers, diminishing the appeal of Instacart." (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0924 ET - Zoom's expansion beyond its video call service to offer a suite of advanced AI tools is paying off, says Benchmark analyst Matthew Harrigan. The company is integrating its agentic AI in its video conferences, call centers and hybrid work products, reducing costs for businesses and leading to high-profile customer wins. "These AI capabilities now extend well beyond Zoom's signature brand name virtual meetings to assimilated information and commands, contextual reasoning and clear follow-up tasks," Harrigan says, adding that Zoom is also integrating these offerings with Microsoft and Google work platforms. Shares rise 3.9% to $76 in pre-market trading. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0828 ET - Amazon moving into the vehicle sales game with Amazon Autos will likely spook listing platforms like Cars.com, True Car and CarGurus. "But it remains too early to assume AMZN is going to take meaningful share," say Davidson analysts Tom White and Wyatt Swanson. Amazon will likely require dealers to pay around $3,000 a month for listings and to use certain financing partners. That could turn off local dealers, who are often distrustful of third-party platforms, especially when they impact their financing revenue. But if Amazon incorporates its auto sales into Prime, shoppers and web traffic could meaningfully shift to Amazon Autos, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0743 ET - Amazon moving into the vehicle sales game with Amazon Autos will likely spook listing platforms like Cars.com, TrueCar and CarGurus. "But it remains too early to assume AMZN is going to take meaningful share," say Davidson analysts Tom White and Wyatt Swanson. Amazon will likely require dealers to pay around $3,000 a month for listings and to use certain financing partners. That could turn off local dealers, who are often distrustful of third-party platforms, especially when they impact their financing revenue. But if Amazon incorporates its auto sales into Prime, shoppers and web traffic could meaningfully shift to Amazon Autos, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0604 ET - Kuaishou Technology's Kling AI could see modest revenue growth in 2H, Barclays analysts say in a research note. Management raised its 2025 revenue forecast for video-generation tool Kling AI to $125 million, implying about $70 million revenue in 2H. The AI tool generated around $35 million revenue in 2Q, becoming the world's highest-revenue video generation platform, the analysts say. 70% of Kling users are outside of China. Barclays raises its 3Q revenue forecast by 1.7%, driven by better-than-expected outlooks for the e-commerce and live-streaming businesses, partly offset by the ads segment. Kuaishou's shares closed 4.4% higher at HK$74.90. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2331 ET - Bilibili's strong gaming revenue growth over the past 12 months was almost entirely driven by a single title, San Mou, which now appears to have peaked, Nomura analysts write in a note. They warn the game could shift from being a growth driver to a drag in the coming quarters. The company has said it has several new titles in the pipeline, though these aren't yet reflected in guidance due to uncertainties around the launch timing, they say. They think that the chances of upcoming titles matching San Mou's success are low. Nomura maintains its neutral rating on the stock and a target price of $25.00. Bilibili's ADRs last closed at $23.76. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2325 ET - Kuaishou Technology's ads business could face headwinds in 2H, Citi analysts say in a research note as they lower the company's 2H ads growth estimate to 13% from high teens previously. They note that unexpected tightening in online payment policies in Brazil has impacted ads spending by companies there. China's food delivery platforms have also spent less on marketing on Kuaishou's short-video app as their price wars have led to rising user traffic, they note. However, Citi expects Kuaishou's self-developed AI video-generation tool Kling to do better. It raises Kling's 2025 revenue estimate to US$125 million after it recorded more than CNY250 million of revenue in 2Q. Citi sees potential upside risks for revenue if traction becomes stronger-than-expected in 2H. Kuaishou's shares are 3.1% higher at HK$74.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2221 ET - Maxis's key uncertainties likely stem from Malaysia's 5G network ownership and rollouts, with some clarity expected by end-2025, CGS International analyst Prem Jearajasingam says in a note. The telco lifted its 2025 capital expenditure guidance to about 1 billion ringgit on new project wins, he notes. "Management expects some resolution, at least on the way forward, for 5G network ownership by end-2025, but provided no details", he adds. The ongoing sale and service tax negotiations on tower leases could trim future earnings if concessions aren't granted, the analyst warns. CGS International raises Maxis's 2025-2027 core earnings forecasts by up to 2.9% on stronger grant income. It raises Maxis's rating to add after recent share-price declines and raises its target price to MYR4.00 from MYR3.93. Shares are 2.6% higher at MYR3.53. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2144 ET - Maxis' earnings may face headwinds if Malaysian telcos move from usage-based billing for 5G access to fixed minimum fees, Kenanga IB analyst Kylie Chan Sze Zan says in a note. The shift has already been adopted by Telekom Malaysia in 1Q and stipulated in the access agreement with Digital Nasional, Malaysia's 5G provider, she notes. She warns Maxis could be more exposed given its smaller subscriber base and lower 5G traffic volumes. Meanwhile, Maxis is eyeing new growth by leveraging its tie-up with China Mobile International to serve Chinese companies in Malaysia, she adds. Kenanga cuts Maxis' target price to MYR3.56 from MYR3.80, but keeps a market perform rating on the stock. Shares are 2.3% higher at MYR3.53. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1921 ET - Intuit is seeing early traction with the artificial-intelligence agents it started offering last month, CEO Sasan Goodarzi says during a call with analysts. The new feature has engaged millions of customers so far and repeat usage rates are significantly above the company's expectations, Goodarzi says. The agents aim to automate workflows, boosting efficiency and growth for Intuit's business customers, he says. The company did not include monetization from the agents in its current guidance, but has high expectations for contributions in future years, he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1203 ET - The results of a U.S. probe into the pharmaceutical sector are imminent, but any potential hits from President Trump's tariffs and drug-price policies seem to be already priced in by Swiss pharma stocks, Vontobel's Stefan Schneider says in a note. Vontobel estimates U.S. drug prices could be cut by between 5% and 33%, but finds that this is already factored into Swiss pharma stock prices. According to Vontobel's analysis, Roche shares could see a movement from 0% in its base-case scenario to a 7% drop in its worst-case scenario, while Novartis shares could either rise or fall by 8%. This means the outcome of the U.S. investigation could also trigger share-price rises as a clearer picture emerges, the analyst says. Shares in Novartis and Roche close 0.9% and 0.4% lower, respectively. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0352 ET - European pharma companies are seeing reduced uncertainty following the joint statement by the EU and U.S. on tariffs, Deutsche Bank Research analysts say in a note. "As we have noted previously, we view better visibility on tariffs as a likely buy trigger for the sector," DB Research says. As a result, the analysts raise their recommendation for the sector to overweight from underweight. This is good news not only for the sector, but for European equities more broadly, they say. The analysts continue to wait for more clarity on Swiss pharma companies. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

2210 ET - The Philippines looks poised to become a regional pharmaceutical hub, supported by recent investments, analysts at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, write in a report. New drugmaker investments are expected to drive growth in the country's pharmaceutical market, they say. AstraZeneca's new pharmaceutical innovation hub will bolster the Philippines' cancer care capabilities, they add. AstraZeneca's investment agreement mirror similar moves by other companies, including Royale Life Pharma. "These recent announcements underscore Philippines' established position as a key pharmaceutical manufacturer in the Southeast Asia region," the analysts say.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2128 ET - Sonic Healthcare is finding earnings growth from its existing stable of businesses elusive, says Barrenjoey. Sonic has spent some A$3.3 billion on acquisitions since FY 2019. Yet analyst Saul Hadassin points out that Sonic's EPS is likely to be A$1.17 in FY 2026, essentially in line with the A$1.15 achieved in FY 2019. "While we don't dismiss the synergy potential from transactions, it appears that other factors (price cuts, quotas, labor cost inflation, rents) have limited the overall earnings growth of the company and return-on-invested-capital expansion," Barrenjoey says. Its FY 2026-FY 2028 EPS forecasts fall by 10% on average. Barrenjoey retains an underweight call on Sonic.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
